name: Coast Guard Sector Miami
tagline: The Coast Guard's South Florida hub for Caribbean operations, migrant and drug interdiction, and rescue.
_note: Coast Guard Sector Miami + Air Station Miami (Opa-locka) (South Florida). CONUS full template (BAH MHA FL061 Miami/Fort Lauderdale; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). CG uses same BAH tables. Caribbean/SE ops: interdiction, SAR, port security. Medical: CG clinic + TRICARE network. Facts verified from official sources (uscg.mil, TRICARE, CGX, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Florida is TRICARE East (no state income tax). Higher-cost South Florida. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 33054 (Opa-locka).

data.Role: South Florida hub for Caribbean operations
data.Mission: Migrant and drug interdiction, search and rescue, and port security
data.Note: Florida has no state income tax

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Coast Guard Sector Miami, with Air Station Miami at Opa-locka, is the service's South Florida hub, central to Caribbean operations: migrant and drug interdiction, search and rescue, and port security across busy, high-stakes waters.
It's in the vibrant, diverse South Florida metro, which brings beaches and culture along with a higher cost of living (offset somewhat by no state income tax).

sections.title: South Florida Living: Vibrant, With Commute and Cost Trade-offs

data.subtitle: South Florida is lively but pricey and traffic-heavy. The main choice is which part of the metro fits your commute, schools, and budget.
intro: Housing costs have risen across South Florida, so where you live is mostly about commute versus price. Coast Guard housing is limited, so most families rent.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across Miami-Dade and into Broward, with traffic a major factor.

recommendations.advice: Areas near Opa-locka and north-central Miami-Dade keep the commute to the air station shorter.
Broward County (Pembroke Pines, Miramar, Cooper City) and suburban Miami-Dade are popular with families for schools.
South Florida traffic is heavy; weigh it carefully against price.
Some military housing exists in the region; check early, but plan to rent.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD/CG ID need to check in at the visitor control point.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Coast Guard site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Coast Guard family housing in South Florida is limited, so most families rent across Miami-Dade and Broward.
Eligibility and availability vary, so start with the Coast Guard housing office for current options and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA FL061 (Miami/Fort Lauderdale) — Coast Guard uses the same BAH tables

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Miami-Dade County and Broward County public schools serve military families depending on where you live.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Clinic: Coast Guard clinic, Sector/Air Station Miami
data.Care: On-base clinic care for the assigned population; most family care is via the TRICARE network and South Florida hospitals

data.Commissary: Nearest DoD commissary is at Homestead ARB (south of Miami)

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and in pricey, fast-moving South Florida the call depends heavily on tour length and budget.
rent: Your tour is a standard length and you'll PCS out.
South Florida prices put a purchase out of comfortable reach for your budget.
You want flexibility in an expensive, fast-moving market.
You'd rather not take on rising coastal insurance as an owner.
buy: You expect a longer tour or repeat South Florida assignments.
You can afford the entry price and want to build equity with your VA loan.
You've found a community and commute you're confident about.
You're comfortable with Florida coastal insurance costs.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer. South Florida's high prices and rising insurance make buying a bigger commitment, so many families rent. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, factor in insurance, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. In a big, busy metro, starting early pays off.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Plan around commute
steps.text: CG housing is limited; pick your area around commute and traffic, and get the housing office's read before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Florida vehicle registration, and learn the visitor process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (Miami-Dade or Broward) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment and pick a network provider; the base clinic serves the assigned population.

data.a: It's the Coast Guard's South Florida hub (with Air Station Miami at Opa-locka) for Caribbean operations: migrant and drug interdiction, search and rescue, and port security.

data.q: Do Coast Guard families get BAH?
data.a: Yes, the same BAH tables as the rest of the military. It's MHA FL061 (Miami/Fort Lauderdale).
Across Miami-Dade and Broward. North-central Miami-Dade is closer to Opa-locka; Broward suburbs are popular for schools. CG housing is limited.
South Florida has gotten pricey and insurance is rising, though there's no state income tax. Commute versus cost drives where you live.
